item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: NavBumba , instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P.S. This one was able to derail my research, good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It made me laugh a bit and was an enjoyable read, but I happened to find this while researching Site-15, which is the only way I noticed this potential mistake. From the page about foundation sites, Site-15 "specializes in the containment and study of electric, electronic, and computer-based anomalies." because of this, I think it's unlikely that a living stapler would be contained at Site-15. If I were to choose the most suitable containment site for SCP-5070, I would choose Site-76, which is used for containing objects that are presumed to be man-made. If objects confirmed to be man made are not contained in Site-76 then there are other miscellaneous sites with no criteria that i could find. but despite this minor mistake I loved it, especially that interview, it was surprisingly engaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well White actually was scouting the site from a distance, he never actually penetrated into the site. I made White having remote viewing was to why he knew where Vamp was being kept, I never intended for it to be a huge part of the article. I'm glad you liked it though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this SCP. The anomaly is simple but also really creative and works in a way that makes sense. It serves as an interesting hook for the article in general, although the storyline doesn't really live up to that. It's a bit of a cliché story and there are some redundant details such as too much talking about White's family background (also, you almost lost my upvote at that "random guy found lurking in Foundation site" part). I still have a soft spot for it though, since it is truly heartwarming and comforting, with a nice happy ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP and great ending +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing that previous comment, this really makes me think of a pocket Marceline, heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the things I love about this site is how emotionally affected I can be about a story of an annoyingly bitey magic stapler and the owner who loves him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah that last line is a typo, I fixed it now. Could have sworn that said SCP-5070, not 1877.

But I'm glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this one made me feel happy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey just wanted to say I loved this. It’s easy going and got a sweet ending but best of all, the characterization of White is terrific.

One tiny thing, the last line might be a typo: “Proposal to allow Po1-1877 to interact with SCP-1877 once every two weeks have been approved.”

Thinking this is meant to be SCP-5070?

Anyway, great stuff! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I thought this story would be best if I ended it at a somewhat happy note. Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having the creator join staff and retain contact with Vamp was a wonderful twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it. I was afraid the concept wasn't going to be received well but I'm glad that's not the case. And I agree with you, Vamp does have that 'cuteness' factor going for him… somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I allowed to say a blood sucking bird stapler is adorable? Fuck it, I’m saying it! This is adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't see the parallels with Marceline until you brought it up. What a coincidence. I'm glad you liked the piece. I always try to aim for simple but meaningful stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5070 is able to use red coloring as a substitute for sustenance. When given objects colored red (or other shades of red), it will approach and 'bite' down on the widest surface possible. The color will recede to where the teeth meet until disappearing completely, leaving neutral colors (white, gray, black etc.) behind. This process only affects the color red; other colors will be unaffected.

This made me remember of Marceline.

Overall, It's a simple yet well written work. Keep up with the good stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I'm sorry if there are some tid-bits in the article the ruined the experience for you. But I did fix some of the points you mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really, really close for me, but I think there a few problems that need ironing out before I could upvote.

+ Some critique
- Some critique

Shortly after capture, it became apparent that the objects that SCP-5070 has "drained" led a trail from the golf course all the way to the suburban home of Dominic White (PoI-1877)

This is a good example of one of areas where I sense a lack of clinical tone. Putting a word in quotation marks that isn't actually a quote is just putting in an off-tone word. Also, the 'all the way' in the same sentence feels unnecessarily opinionated.

In the interview log, some of the dialogue could use another pass. I noticed the POI strangely seems to avoid using pronouns when referring to Vamp. "he" was used a few times, so it's not a gender problem. When the subject is obvious, English speakers tend to use pronouns much more often. If this was intentional, the rest of the POI's dialogue didn't cue me to this person having a particularly strange speech pattern to justify it, just a few little things like lack of contractions that could also just be chalked up to stilted writing.

These are just a sample of the issues I see with the execution of the article. I see potential in the theme, even the narrative, but I can't upvote in the article's current state, I'm taken out of the narrative too often. Potential though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP quite a bit. It's an interesting idea, written well, and tells a nice story. I also like the ending quite a bit, you've got my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh! thanks, sorry about that. I was a bit tired when I made this. It's fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have An SCP-XXXX in the image caption, otherwise great read my guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was certainly a pleasure for me too. I'll be sure to give you the credit today. Thanks so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s here! Was a pleasure critting this and I’m glad the little stapler’s finally on the site.

item.title: Re: SCP-5070
item.content:encoded: I didn't think a theme was necessary for this one. This isn't a part of the 2020 canon contest. But I appreciate the criticism

item.title: SCP-5070
item.content:encoded: Good writing, using a header or a theme could help the presentation.

item.title: AUTHOR POST: Lost Roads and Fading Paw Prints.
item.content:encoded:
Wow, second scp, I'm really getting the hang of this. I created this SCP for two reasons, one, for my dog that sadly passed away from cancer, she helped me a lot through tough times, it'll won't be the same without her. It feels like I've been cheated, I can't explain it.

Two, I've been struggling what I really want ever since I reached my teens. This pandemic just really puts things in perspective. But I'll think I'll find my path one day. It just scares me that I might make a mistake I can't come back from. But I'm doing good so far I suppose.

I want to thank DrDromeus for helping out with this piece. He pointed out a lot of good points and helped me craft the draft. Thanks.

The image that is used in the article was created and owned by me. I made some wings and taped it on the stapler.
